input:
Rania Ahmed
Talend ETL Developer
rania.ahmed@email.com |+20 101 234 5678 |/githubgithub.com/rania-ahmed |/linkedinlinkedin.com/in/rania-ahmed |
raniaahmed.com
Education
University of Mumbai
Bachelor of Engineering (BE) January 2012 - January 2016, Mumbai, Maharashtra, India
Experience
Talend ETL Developer Full-time
Tata Consultancy Services January 2016 - Present, Cairo, Egypt
•Prepared ETL mapping Documents for every mapping and Data Migration document for smooth transfer of project
from development to testing environment and then to production environment.
•Performed Unit testing and System testing to validate data loads in the target. Troubleshoot long running jobs and
fixed the issues.
•Expertise in creating mappings in TALEND using Big Data supporting components such as tJDBCConfiguration,
tJDBCInput, tHDFSConfiguration, tS3configuration, tCacheOut, tCacheIn, tSqlRow and standard components like
tFileInputDelimited, tFileOutputDelimited, tMap, tJoin, tReplicate, tParallelize, tConvertType, tAggregate, tSortRow,
tFlowMeter, tLogCatcher, tRowGenerator, tJava, tJavarow, tAggregateRow, tFilter etc.
•Used ETL methodologies and best practices to create Talend ETL jobs. Followed and enhanced programming and
naming standards. Developed jobs, components and Joblets in Talend. Used tRunJob component to run child job
from a parent job and to pass parameters from parent to child job.
•Created and deployed physical objects including custom tables, custom views, stored procedures, and indexes to
SQL server for Staging and Data-Warehouse environment. Involved in writing SQL Queries and used Joins to access
data from MySQL.
•Created and managed Source to Target mapping documents for all Facts & Dimension tables. Broad design, devel-
opment and testing experience with Talend Integration Suite and knowledge in Performance Tuning of mappings.
•Extensively used tMap component which does lookup & Joiner Functions. Experienced in writing expressions within
tMap as per the business need. Handled insert and update Strategy using tSQLRow.
•Created Implicit, local and global Context variables in the job to run Talend jobs against different environments.
•Worked on Talend Administration Console (TAC) for scheduling jobs and adding users. Experienced in Building a
Talend job outside of a Talend studio as well as on TAC server.
•Developed mappings to load Fact and Dimension tables, SCD Type 1 and SCD Type 2 dimensions and Incremental
loading and unit tested the mappings.
•Developed Framework Integrated Job which schedules multiple jobs at a time and updates the last successful run
time, success status, sending mail for failed jobs, maintaining the counts in SQL Database. Used tParallelize compo-
nent and multi thread execution option to run subjobs in parallel which increas

es the performance of a job.
•Created Talend jobs to copy the files from one server to another and utilized Talend FTP components. Implemented
FTP operations using Talend Studio to transfer files in between network folders as well as to FTP server using
components like tFileList, tS3Put, tFTPut, tFileExist, tFTPConnection etc.
•Extracted data from flat files/databases applied business logic to load them in the staging database as well as flat
files.
•Successfully Loaded Data into different targets from various source systems like SQL Database, DB2, Flatfiles, XML
files etc. into the Staging table and then to the target database.
•Experience in development and design of ETL (Extract, Transform and Loading data) methodology for supporting
data transformations and processing, in a corporate wide ETL Solution using TALEND Big Data Platform.
•Excellent working experience in Agile methodologies.
•Proficiency in gathering and understanding the client requirements and translate business needs into technical re-
quirements.
•Design and develop end-to-end ETL process from various source systems to Staging area, from staging to Data
Warehouse, soliciting and documenting business, functional and data requirements, context/variable diagrams, use
cases and ETL related diagrams.

•Excellent oral/written communication with ability to effectively work with onsite and remote teams.
•A good team player with excellent problem solving ability and time management skills having profound insight to
determine priorities, schedule work and meet critical deadlines.
Skills
ETL Tools: Talend Big Data - Informatica PowerCenter - Talend Administration Console (TAC)
Databases: Microsoft SQL Server - SQL - Redshift - MySQL - DB2 - Microsoft Access
Cloud Platforms: AWS Services (S3, Athena)
Data Warehousing: Data Warehouse Concept (Star Schema, Facts, Dimensions) - Data Modeling - Data
Integration
Development Tools: Microsoft Visual Studio - SQL Platform 6.2.1 Management Studio Workbench
Methodologies: Agile
Rania Ahmed is a Talend ETL Developer with contact information including an email address, phone number, GitHub profile, LinkedIn profile, and personal website.
Rania Ahmed received a Bachelor of Engineering (BE) from the University of Mumbai between January 2012 and January 2016, located in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

Rania Ahmed has been working as a Talend ETL Developer at Tata Consultancy Services since January 2016 in Cairo, Egypt, where she prepares ETL mapping documents, performs unit and system testing, and troubleshoots data loads.

Rania Ahmed has expertise in creating mappings in Talend using various Big Data components like tJDBCConfiguration, tHDFSConfiguration, tS3configuration, and standard components like tFileInputDelimited, tMap, tJoin, and others, while adhering to ETL methodologies and best practices.

Rania Ahmed has experience creating and deploying physical objects like custom tables, views, stored procedures, and indexes to SQL server for staging and data warehousing, and writing SQL queries with joins to access data from MySQL.

Rania Ahmed created and managed source-to-target mapping documents for facts and dimension tables, performed broad design, development, and testing with Talend Integration Suite, and tuned performance of mappings. She also extensively used the tMap component for lookup and joiner functions.

Rania Ahmed created implicit, local, and global context variables in Talend jobs to run them against different environments and worked on Talend Administration Console (TAC) for scheduling jobs and adding users.

Rania Ahmed developed mappings to load fact and dimension tables, SCD Type 1 and SCD Type 2 dimensions, and incremental loading, and unit tested the mappings. She also developed a framework integrated job that schedules multiple jobs, updates runtime status, sends failure emails, and maintains counts in a SQL database.
Rania Ahmed used the tParallelize component and multi-thread execution to run subjobs in parallel, increasing performance.

The candidate created Talend jobs to copy files between servers using Talend FTP components, including tFileList, tS3Put, tFTPut, tFileExist, and tFTPConnection, to transfer files between network folders and FTP servers.
Data was extracted from flat files and databases, business logic was applied, and the data was loaded into a staging database and flat files.
Data was successfully loaded from various sources like SQL Database, DB2, Flatfiles, and XML files into a staging table and then to the target database.
The candidate has experience in developing and designing ETL methodology using the TALEND Big Data Platform to support data transformations and processing.
The candidate possesses excellent working experience in Agile methodologies and proficiency in gathering and understanding client requirements, translating business needs into technical requirements.

The candidate can design and develop end-to-end ETL processes from various source systems to a staging area and then to a Data Warehouse, including documenting business, functional, and data requirements, as well as ETL diagrams.
The candidate has excellent communication skills and is a good team player with strong problem-solving and time management skills.

The candidate's skills include ETL Tools (Talend Big Data, Informatica PowerCenter, Talend Administration Console), Databases (Microsoft SQL Server, SQL, Redshift, MySQL, DB2, Microsoft Access), Cloud Platforms (AWS Services - S3, Athena), and Data Warehousing concepts.

Rania Ahmed is a meticulous and resourceful Talend ETL Developer currently based in Cairo, Egypt, with Tata Consultancy Services. A graduate of the University of Mumbai – where she earned her Bachelor of Engineering between 2012 and 2016 – she brings a strong foundation in data engineering principles to her work. Rania excels at translating data requirements into robust ETL processes, crafting detailed source-to-target mapping documents and expertly utilizing Talend Integration Suite. 

She’s fluent in a wide array of Talend components, from Big Data connectors like tJDBCConfiguration, tHDFSConfiguration, and tS3configuration, to core components such as tFileInputDelimited, tMap, and tJoin. Rania doesn’t just build pipelines; she optimizes them, demonstrating a keen eye for performance tuning and leveraging techniques like multi-thread execution with the tParallelize component. 

Beyond Talend, she’s proficient in SQL, capable of designing and deploying database objects – tables, views, stored procedures, and indexes – to SQL Server, and querying data from MySQL. Rania is also adept at managing data warehousing concepts, including SCD Type 1 and Type 2 dimensions, and implementing incremental loading strategies. She’s a proactive problem-solver, comfortable troubleshooting data loads and ensuring data integrity. A dedicated professional, she also demonstrates administrative skills through her work with Talend Administration Console (TAC), scheduling jobs and managing user access. You can find her work and connect with her via rania.ahmed@email.com, +20 101 234 5678, githubgithub.com/rania-ahmed, linkedin.com/in/rania-ahmed, and raniaahmed.com.

Talend is a powerful, versatile integration platform – a digital workhorse for moving and transforming data. It’s less a single tool and more a comprehensive ecosystem, capable of orchestrating complex data flows between disparate systems. Think of it as a meticulous architect, building pipelines to connect everything from traditional databases to the sprawling landscapes of Big Data – Hadoop, S3, and beyond. 

Talend excels at ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, ensuring data is not just moved, but *refined* along the way. Skilled practitioners, like Rania Ahmed, wield its components – tJDBCConfiguration for database connections, tHDFSConfiguration for Hadoop integration, tS3configuration for cloud storage – with precision, crafting mappings that cleanse, reshape, and enrich information. 

It’s a platform built for practical tasks: reliably copying files between servers, leveraging FTP components like tFileList and tFTPut to bridge network folders and remote servers. Talend doesn’t just *handle* data; it *manages* it, creating repeatable, robust Jobs that automate critical data operations. It’s the engine behind countless data-driven processes, quietly ensuring information flows where it needs to be, when it needs to be.

MySQL is a robust and widely-used relational database management system, a cornerstone for applications demanding reliable data storage and retrieval. It’s a pragmatic choice, frequently found powering everything from small personal projects to large-scale enterprise systems. Though comfortable alongside more complex database solutions like Redshift and DB2, MySQL distinguishes itself with accessibility and speed. Professionals like Rania Ahmed demonstrate proficiency in extracting and manipulating data *from* MySQL using SQL, often integrating it into broader data warehousing pipelines built with tools like Talend and Informatica. It’s a key component in the data ecosystem, frequently leveraged for staging and reporting, and a familiar face for those working with SQL Server, AWS, and a variety of ETL processes. While not explicitly a cloud platform itself, MySQL integrates seamlessly with cloud services like AWS S3 and Athena, extending its reach and utility. It’s a workhorse database, valued for its versatility and the ease with which data can be accessed and transformed.

The Talend Administration Console – often called TAC – is the central nervous system for a Talend data integration ecosystem. It’s a robust, web-based interface designed for orchestrating and managing the flow of data across an organization. Think of it as the control room where complex ETL processes are brought to life and meticulously monitored. 

TAC isn’t about *building* the data pipelines themselves, but rather about deploying, scheduling, and securing them. Users like Rania Ahmed leverage it to bring Talend Jobs – those intricate recipes for data transformation – into production, tailoring their execution to specific environments through the clever use of context variables. It’s the place to define who has access to what, adding and managing users to ensure data governance and security. 

Essentially, TAC transforms individual Talend Jobs from isolated creations into a coordinated, enterprise-level data integration solution. It’s a key component within a broader skillset encompassing ETL tools, database management, and increasingly, cloud platforms like AWS, allowing organizations to reliably move and process data at scale. It’s the engine that keeps the data flowing, and the guardian of its integrity.

DB2 is a robust and versatile database management system, a workhorse for organizations needing to reliably store and access critical information. Often found at the heart of large-scale data operations, it’s a seasoned player comfortable ingesting data from a diverse range of origins – SQL databases, flat files, even XML streams – and seamlessly integrating with modern ETL processes using tools like Talend and Informatica. While it stands confidently alone, DB2 frequently collaborates within a broader data ecosystem, acting as a key source for data warehousing initiatives and existing alongside other database technologies like SQL Server, MySQL, and Redshift. It’s a dependable foundation, proven in handling substantial data loads and a cornerstone skill for data professionals building and maintaining complex data pipelines.

The SQL Database stands as a central repository, a quiet workhorse diligently receiving and organizing information from a diverse network of sources. It isn’t flashy, but it’s foundational – the bedrock upon which critical processes are built. Rania Ahmed, a meticulous architect of data flow, has woven intricate pathways to feed it life. She’s crafted the logic to ingest facts and dimensions, handling both simple updates and the complexities of tracking historical changes with SCD Type 1 and Type 2 dimensions. Her work ensures the database isn’t merely a container, but a dynamic record of evolving information. 

Data streams in from a variety of origins: fellow SQL Databases, the legacy systems of DB2, the straightforward clarity of flatfiles, and the structured complexity of XML. All converge first in a staging area, a temporary holding space, before being carefully curated and loaded into the database’s core. 

But the database isn’t passive. It’s actively monitored by a Framework Integrated Job, a sophisticated scheduler that relies on the database to report its health. This job doesn’t just run tasks; it *knows* when things go wrong, sending out alerts and meticulously tracking progress. The database, in turn, provides the vital status updates that keep the entire system informed. It’s a reciprocal relationship – the database provides the data, and receives the oversight necessary to maintain its integrity. Ultimately, it serves as the authoritative source for this data, a trusted record of operations and insights.

Databases are the silent architects of modern information, vast and organized repositories where raw data finds structure and meaning. They are not monolithic entities, but rather a diverse family – from the robust, enterprise-grade Microsoft SQL Server and the venerable DB2, to the nimble MySQL and the cloud-powered Redshift, even extending to the accessible familiarity of Microsoft Access. Each possesses unique strengths, yet all share the core purpose of collecting, storing, and retrieving information with efficiency and precision. 

They are the foundational source of data, the wellspring from which insights flow. Often populated through intricate processes – the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of information from flat files and other sources – databases are rarely static. Skilled hands, wielding tools like Talend and Informatica, shape and refine the data within, preparing it for analysis and application. Increasingly, these databases find a home in the cloud, leveraging platforms like AWS and services like S3 and Athena to scale and adapt to ever-growing demands.  At their heart, they all speak the language of SQL, a universal dialect for querying and manipulating the information they hold. They are the indispensable backbone of any data-driven endeavor.
